transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Good morning, First National Bank. My name is Luke. Can I take your full name and date of birth?

transcript.speaker: customer
transcript.text: Morning. It's Claire Donohue, eighteenth of September 1992.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Thank you Claire, you're verified. What can I help with today?

transcript.speaker: customer
transcript.text: My card was declined this morning at a garage when I tried to pay for petrol. It's embarrassing — I have money in my account.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: I'm sorry that happened. Let me check the card and account status now. Your balance is healthy and the card is active. The declined transaction — the garage attempted to pre-authorise one hundred and fifty pounds, which is a common fuel station practice to reserve funds. Your available balance at the time was ninety-eight pounds, which fell below their pre-auth threshold.

transcript.speaker: customer
transcript.text: Oh — I had no idea that was a thing. So it was refused because of a temporary hold, not because I actually don't have the money?

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Exactly right. The actual cost of your fuel would have been far less, but the pre-auth check fails first. You can pay inside at the counter rather than at the pump to avoid this — inside payments use the exact amount.

transcript.speaker: customer
transcript.text: That's really useful to know. Is there anything you need to do on your end?

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Nothing at all — your card and account are completely fine. Is there anything else I can help with?

transcript.speaker: customer
transcript.text: No, that makes complete sense now. Thank you Luke.
